channel.title: family-friendly-meat-dishes posts - RSS Feed
channel.description: Fresh family-friendly-meat-dishes posts and cooking inspiration

item.title: Cozy Slow Cooker Beef Stew That Melts Hearts
item.description: This slow cooker beef stew is pure comfort in a bowl. With tender meat and hearty veggies, it's perfect for chilly evenings. Just set it and forget it!
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 480 minutes

⏰ Total Time: 495 minutes

🍽️ Servings: 6

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 2 pounds beef chuck, cut into 1-inch cubes
  • 4 cups beef broth
  • 3 medium carrots, sliced
  • 3 medium potatoes, diced
  • 1 onion, chopped
  • 3 cloves garlic, minced
  • 2 tablespoons tomato paste
  • 1 teaspoon dried thyme
  • 1 teaspoon dried rosemary
  • Salt and pepper to taste
  • 2 tablespoons olive oil

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Brown the beef cubes on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
  2. Transfer the browned beef to the slow cooker. Add the chopped onion, garlic, carrots, and potatoes.
  3. In a bowl, mix the beef broth, tomato paste, thyme, rosemary, salt, and pepper. Pour this mixture over the beef and vegetables.
  4. Cover and cook on low for 8 hours or high for 4 hours, until the beef is tender.
  5. Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary before serving.

📌 Pinterest Tags: #cat1761229889885 #Easypost #post #cooking #foodie #homemade #delicious #chuck #broth #medium

📌 Save this post to Pinterest!

Perfect for meal planning and sharing with friends

media:content.media:title: Cozy Slow Cooker Beef Stew That Melts Hearts
media:content.media:description: This slow cooker beef stew is pure comfort in a bowl. With tender meat and hearty veggies, it's perfect for chilly evenings. Just set it and forget it!

media:group.media:keywords: cat1761229889885, Easypost, post, cooking, foodie, homemade, delicious, chuck, broth, medium

item.title: Best Homemade Chicken Pot Pie: Comfort in Every Bite
item.description: This homemade chicken pot pie is the ultimate comfort food. Loaded with tender chicken and veggies in a creamy sauce, it’s perfect for cozy dinners or family gatherings!
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 20 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 45 minutes

⏰ Total Time: 65 minutes

🍽️ Servings: 6

📊 Difficulty: Medium

🥘 Ingredients

  • 1 lb chicken breast, cooked and shredded
  • 1 cup carrots, diced
  • 1 cup peas, frozen
  • 1/2 cup celery, diced
  • 1/2 cup onion, chopped
  • 1/3 cup butter
  • 1/3 cup all-purpose flour
  • 1 3/4 cups chicken broth
  • 2/3 cup milk
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1/2 tsp black pepper
  • 1 tsp thyme
  • 2 pie crusts (store-bought or homemade)

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C).
  2. In a large skillet, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the onions, carrots, and celery. Sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
  3. Stir in the flour, salt, pepper, and thyme. Cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly.
  4. Gradually whisk in the chicken broth and milk. Cook until the mixture thickens, about 5 minutes.
  5. Add the shredded chicken and peas to the skillet, mixing well. Remove from heat.
  6. Place one pie crust in a 9-inch pie dish. Pour the chicken mixture into the crust. Cover with the second crust, sealing the edges.
  7. Cut a few slits in the top crust to allow steam to escape. Bake for 30-35 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown.

📌 Pinterest Tags: #cat1761229889885 #Mediumpost #post #cooking #foodie #homemade #delicious #chicken #carrots #frozen

📌 Save this post to Pinterest!

Perfect for meal planning and sharing with friends

media:content.media:description: This homemade chicken pot pie is the ultimate comfort food. Loaded with tender chicken and veggies in a creamy sauce, it’s perfect for cozy dinners or family gatherings!

item.title: Perfect Meatloaf and Mashed Potatoes: A Comfort Dinner to Remember
item.description: This classic meatloaf paired with creamy mashed potatoes is pure comfort food. It's hearty, satisfying, and perfect for family dinners on chilly nights!
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 60 minutes

⏰ Total Time: 75 minutes

🍽️ Servings: 6

📊 Difficulty: Medium

🥘 Ingredients

  • 1 1/2 pounds ground beef
  • 1 cup breadcrumbs
  • 1/2 cup chopped onion
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced
  • 1/4 cup milk
  • 1/4 cup ketchup
  • 1 egg
  • 1 teaspoon salt
  • 1/2 teaspoon pepper
  • 1 teaspoon Worcestershire sauce
  • 2 pounds potatoes, peeled and cubed
  • 1/2 cup butter
  • 1/2 cup heavy cream
  • Salt and pepper to taste

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C).
  2. In a large bowl, mix together the ground beef, breadcrumbs, onion, garlic, milk, ketchup, egg, salt, pepper, and Worcestershire sauce until well combined.
  3. Transfer the meat mixture to a loaf pan and shape it into a loaf. Spread a little extra ketchup on top for a glaze.
  4. Bake in the preheated oven for about 1 hour, or until the internal temperature reaches 160°F (70°C).
  5. While the meatloaf is baking, place the cubed potatoes in a large pot of salted water. Bring to a boil and cook until tender, about 15-20 minutes.
  6. Drain the potatoes and return them to the pot. Add butter, heavy cream, salt, and pepper. Mash until smooth and creamy.
  7. Serve slices of meatloaf alongside a generous scoop of mashed potatoes.

📌 Pinterest Tags: #cat1761229889885 #Mediumpost #post #cooking #foodie #homemade #delicious #ground #breadcrumbs #chopped

📌 Save this post to Pinterest!

Perfect for meal planning and sharing with friends

media:content.media:description: This classic meatloaf paired with creamy mashed potatoes is pure comfort food. It's hearty, satisfying, and perfect for family dinners on chilly nights!

item.title: Comforting Meatloaf and Creamy Mashed Potatoes for Dinner
item.description: This classic meatloaf paired with creamy mashed potatoes is pure comfort food. It's hearty, satisfying, and perfect for family dinners or cozy nights in!
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 60 minutes

⏰ Total Time: 75 minutes

🍽️ Servings: 6

📊 Difficulty: Medium

🥘 Ingredients

  • 1 ½ lbs ground beef
  • 1 cup breadcrumbs
  • 1 onion, finely chopped
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced
  • 1 egg, beaten
  • 1 cup milk
  • 2 tbsp Worcestershire sauce
  • 1 tsp salt
  • ½ tsp black pepper
  • 1 cup ketchup (for glaze)
  • 2 lbs potatoes, peeled and diced
  • ½ cup butter
  • ½ cup heavy cream
  • Salt and pepper to taste

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C).
  2. In a large bowl, mix together the ground beef, breadcrumbs, onion, garlic, egg, milk, Worcestershire sauce, salt, and pepper until well combined.
  3. Transfer the meat mixture into a loaf pan, shaping it into a loaf. Spread ketchup over the top for a nice glaze.
  4. Bake in the preheated oven for about 50-60 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 160°F (70°C).
  5. While the meatloaf is baking, boil the diced potatoes in salted water until tender, about 15-20 minutes.
  6. Drain the potatoes and return them to the pot. Add butter, heavy cream, and season with salt and pepper. Mash until creamy.
  7. Once the meatloaf is done, let it rest for 10 minutes before slicing. Serve with a generous scoop of mashed potatoes.

📌 Pinterest Tags: #cat1761229889885 #Mediumpost #post #cooking #foodie #homemade #delicious #ground #breadcrumbs #onion

📌 Save this post to Pinterest!

Perfect for meal planning and sharing with friends

item.title: The Best Homemade Chicken Pot Pie You'll Ever Make
item.description: This homemade chicken pot pie is pure comfort in a flaky crust. Filled with tender chicken and veggies, it's perfect for family dinners or a cozy night in!
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 30 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 45 minutes

⏰ Total Time: 75 minutes

🍽️ Servings: 6

📊 Difficulty: Medium

🥘 Ingredients

  • 1 lb boneless, skinless chicken breasts, diced
  • 1 cup carrots, diced
  • 1 cup peas, frozen or fresh
  • 1 cup celery, diced
  • 1/2 cup onion, chopped
  • 3 cups chicken broth
  • 1/2 cup milk
  • 1/4 cup butter
  • 1/3 cup all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp garlic powder
  • 1 tsp thyme
  • Salt and pepper to taste
  • 2 pie crusts (store-bought or homemade)

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C).
  2. In a large pot, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the onions, carrots, and celery, cooking until softened, about 5 minutes.
  3. Stir in the chicken and cook until no longer pink, about 5-7 minutes. Sprinkle in the flour and stir well.
  4. Gradually add the chicken broth and milk, stirring constantly until the mixture thickens, about 5 minutes. Add the peas, garlic powder, thyme, salt, and pepper.
  5. Pour the chicken mixture into a pie dish lined with one pie crust. Cover with the second crust and cut slits for steam to escape.
  6. Bake for 30-35 minutes or until the crust is golden brown. Let it cool for a few minutes before serving.

📌 Pinterest Tags: #cat1761229889885 #Mediumpost #post #cooking #foodie #homemade #delicious #boneless #carrots #frozen

📌 Save this post to Pinterest!

Perfect for meal planning and sharing with friends

item.title: Red Wine Braised Short Ribs: A Romantic Dinner Delight
item.description: These Red Wine Braised Short Ribs are a showstopper for any romantic dinner. Slow-cooked to perfection in a rich, velvety sauce, they’re sure to impress! Serve with mashed potatoes for a cozy night in.
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 180 minutes

⏰ Total Time: 195 minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Medium

🥘 Ingredients

  • 4 lbs bone-in beef short ribs
  • Salt and pepper, to taste
  • 2 tablespoons olive oil
  • 1 large onion, chopped
  • 2 carrots, chopped
  • 2 celery stalks, chopped
  • 4 cloves garlic, minced
  • 2 cups red wine (like Cabernet Sauvignon)
  • 4 cups beef broth
  • 2 sprigs fresh thyme
  • 1 bay leaf

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Preheat your oven to 325°F (160°C).
  2. Season the short ribs generously with salt and pepper.
  3. In a large Dutch oven, heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Sear the short ribs on all sides until browned, about 3-4 minutes per side. Remove and set aside.
  4. In the same pot, add onion, carrots, and celery. Sauté until softened, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in garlic and cook for another minute.
  5. Pour in the red wine, scraping up any brown bits from the bottom. Bring to a simmer and let reduce by half.
  6. Return the short ribs to the pot, add beef broth, thyme, and bay leaf. Bring to a boil, then cover and transfer to the oven.
  7. Braise for about 2.5 to 3 hours, until the meat is tender and falling off the bone. Remove from the oven and let rest for 10 minutes before serving.

📌 Pinterest Tags: #cat1761229889885 #Mediumpost #post #cooking #foodie #homemade #delicious #bonein #pepper #olive

📌 Save this post to Pinterest!

Perfect for meal planning and sharing with friends

item.title: Crispy Air Fryer Brussels Sprouts with Parmesan: A Game-Changer!
item.description: These crispy Brussels sprouts are a delightful side dish that’s ready in just 20 minutes! Tossed with Parmesan for a savory crunch, they’re perfect for weeknight dinners or holiday feasts.
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 15 minutes

⏰ Total Time: 30 minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 1 pound Brussels sprouts, trimmed and halved
  • 2 tablespoons olive oil
  • 1 teaspoon garlic powder
  • 1/2 teaspoon salt
  • 1/4 teaspoon black pepper
  • 1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Preheat your air fryer to 375°F (190°C).
  2. In a large bowl, toss the halved Brussels sprouts with olive oil, garlic powder, salt, and pepper until evenly coated.
  3. Place the Brussels sprouts in the air fryer basket in a single layer. Cook for 10 minutes.
  4. After 10 minutes, shake the basket to ensure even cooking, then sprinkle the Parmesan cheese over the sprouts.
  5. Cook for an additional 5 minutes, or until the sprouts are crispy and the cheese is golden.
  6. Remove from the air fryer and serve immediately, garnished with extra Parmesan if desired.

📌 Pinterest Tags: #cat1761229889885 #Easypost #quickpost #30minutemeals #post #cooking #foodie #homemade #delicious #pound #olive #teaspoon

📌 Save this post to Pinterest!

item.title: Hearty Beef Stew That Will Warm Your Soul
item.description: This beef stew is super comforting and ready in about an hour! Packed with tender beef, hearty vegetables, and a rich broth, it's a great choice for chilly nights. Serve it with crusty bread for the ultimate cozy dinner experience. Trust me, you'll love it!
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 45 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 6

📊 Difficulty: Medium

🥘 Ingredients

  • 2 pounds beef chuck, cut into 1-inch cubes
  • 3 tablespoons olive oil
  • 1 large onion, diced
  • 4 cloves garlic, minced
  • 4 cups beef broth
  • 2 cups carrots, sliced
  • 2 cups potatoes, diced
  • 2 cups celery, chopped
  • 2 tablespoons tomato paste
  • 1 tablespoon Worcestershire sauce
  • 1 teaspoon dried thyme
  • Salt and pepper to taste
  • Fresh parsley for garnish

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. First off, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Add the beef cubes and brown them on all sides. This usually takes about 5-7 minutes. Make sure to season with a bit of salt and pepper.
  2. Once the beef is browned, remove it from the pot and set it aside. In the same pot, add the last tablespoon of olive oil and toss in the diced onion. Cook until it’s softened, around 3 minutes, then stir in the garlic and let it cook for another minute.
  3. Now, it’s time to bring everything together! Add the beef back into the pot along with the beef broth, carrots, potatoes, celery, tomato paste, Worcestershire sauce, thyme, and a dash more salt and pepper. Bring this to a boil.
  4. Reduce the heat to low and cover the pot. Let it simmer for about 30-35 minutes, or until the veggies are tender. Give it a stir every now and then – no biggie if you forget!
  5. Check the seasoning before serving. You might wanna add more salt or pepper depending on your taste. Garnish with fresh parsley right before serving.

📌 Pinterest Tags: #cat1761229889885 #Mediumpost #1hourmeals #post #cooking #foodie #homemade #delicious #chuck #olive #large

📌 Save this post to Pinterest!

Perfect for meal planning and sharing with friends

media:content.media:description: This beef stew is super comforting and ready in about an hour! Packed with tender beef, hearty vegetables, and a rich broth, it's a great choice for chilly nights. Serve it with crusty bread for the ultimate cozy dinner experience. Trust me, you'll love it!

item.title: Creamy Slow Cooker Chicken Thighs Recipe
item.description: Creamy slow cooker chicken thighs that practically melt in your mouth, all while you chill out! Featuring tender meat and a silky sauce. Perfect for cozy dinners or meal prep. Save this recipe to enjoy later!
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 6 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 4 bone-in chicken thighs, skin removed
  • 1 cup chicken broth
  • 1 cup heavy cream (I use Organic Valley)
  • 1 onion, chopped
  • 3 cloves garlic, minced
  • 1 tablespoon fresh thyme or 1 teaspoon dried
  • Salt and pepper to taste

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Season the chicken thighs with salt and pepper. Heat a skillet over medium heat. Sear the chicken thighs for about 4-5 minutes on each side until browned. This step? Totally worth it.
  2. While the chicken is browning, combine the chopped onion, minced garlic, chicken broth, and thyme in the slow cooker. Mix it up nicely.
  3. Once the chicken is browned, add it to the slow cooker, pressing it down into the broth mixture. The broth should cover them about halfway. Cover and cook on low for 6 hours.
  4. About 30 minutes before serving, stir in the heavy cream. This is when the magic happens... you’ll want to taste it. Seriously.
  5. When you're ready to eat, the chicken should be super tender. Shred it right in the pot or serve the thighs whole. Either way, it’s gonna be good.

📌 Pinterest Tags: #cat1761229889885 #Easypost #quickpost #30minutemeals #post #cooking #foodie #homemade #delicious #bonein #chicken #heavy

📌 Save this post to Pinterest!

Perfect for meal planning and sharing with friends

item.title: Meatloaf and Creamy Mashed Potatoes: A Weeknight Dinner That Actually Works
item.description: A straightforward meatloaf recipe that stays moist and flavorful, paired with properly creamy mashed potatoes. Learn the techniques that prevent dry meatloaf, how to achieve the right texture balance, and why this method produces consistent results every time.
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 30 minutes

⏰ Total Time: 45 minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 1 lb (450g) ground beef, 80/20 blend
  • 1 cup (120g) breadcrumbs, preferably panko
  • 1/2 cup (80g) yellow onion, finely chopped
  • 1/2 cup (120ml) ketchup, divided
  • 1 large egg, beaten
  • 1 tsp (5g) garlic powder
  • 1 tsp (6g) fine sea salt
  • 1/2 tsp (1g) black pepper
  • 2 lbs (900g) russet potatoes, peeled and quartered
  • 1/2 cup (120ml) whole milk, room temperature
  • 4 tbsp (57g) unsalted butter
  • Salt and pepper to taste for finishing

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. 1 Set your oven to 350°F and let it preheat for 10 minutes. This temperature allows the center of the meatloaf to reach 160°F without the exterior overcooking. Higher temperatures create a crust before the inside is done; lower temperatures take too long and dry out the meat. 10 minutes Oven thermometer reads 350°F, rack is in center position
  2. 2 Chop the onion as finely as you can manage—small pieces distribute better and don't create pockets of raw onion flavor. Put the ground beef, breadcrumbs, chopped onion, egg, 6 tablespoons of the ketchup (reserve 2 tablespoons for the glaze), garlic powder, salt, and pepper into a large bowl. Use your hands to mix everything together, working quickly and gently for about 30-45 seconds. You'll feel when it's combined—there won't be any visible streaks of unmixed ingredients. Stop as soon as it's uniform. Overmixing develops the gluten in the breadcrumbs and creates a tough, dense texture instead of the tender result you want. 2-3 minutes Mixture is uniform in color, no streaks of unmixed beef or egg visible, feels slightly tacky to touch
  3. 3 Shape the mixture into a compact loaf about 8 inches long and place it in a 9x5 inch baking dish. The egg and breadcrumbs act as binders, so it should hold together easily without falling apart. Spread the reserved 2 tablespoons of ketchup across the top in a thin, even layer. As the meatloaf bakes, the sugars in this ketchup will caramelize and form a slightly sticky glaze that seals in moisture and adds a sweet-tangy flavor to the top. 2 minutes Loaf is symmetrical and compact, ketchup layer is thin and even across entire surface
  4. 4 Put the baking dish on the middle rack of your preheated oven. Bake for 25 minutes, then check the internal temperature by inserting an instant-read thermometer into the thickest part of the loaf. You're looking for exactly 160°F. If it's at 158°F, give it another 2 minutes and check again. If it's at 160°F, pull it out immediately. Do not let it reach 165°F—those extra five degrees will make the meat noticeably drier because the protein contracts more tightly. The surface should look slightly bronzed and the ketchup glaze should be darkened but not burnt. 25-30 minutes Instant-read thermometer reads exactly 160°F in center, surface is bronzed, ketchup glaze is caramelized and slightly sticky
  5. 5 While the meatloaf bakes, quarter your peeled potatoes and put them in a large pot. Cover them completely with cold water and add about 1 teaspoon of salt. Bring the water to a rolling boil over high heat, then reduce to medium-high to maintain a steady but not aggressive boil. The potatoes need 15-20 minutes from the moment the water reaches a full boil. Start checking at 15 minutes by piercing one with a fork—it should slide through with almost no resistance. When you lift a piece out with a slotted spoon, it should feel light and almost fall apart. If there's any firmness in the center, keep boiling for another 2-3 minutes and check again. 15-20 minutes Fork slides through potato with zero resistance, potato feels floury and light when lifted, centers are completely tender
  6. 6 Drain the potatoes in a colander and immediately return them to the warm pot, which you've set on a turned-off burner. Add the butter and milk—both should be close to room temperature or slightly warmed. If you add cold dairy, it cools the potatoes and makes them harder to mash smoothly. Use a potato masher and press down firmly, working around the pot to break down each piece. Continue mashing for 1-2 minutes until you reach a creamy consistency with just a few small lumps remaining. If you prefer completely smooth potatoes, switch to a hand mixer on low speed for 30 seconds, but don't overdo it or you'll release too much starch and create a gluey paste. Season with salt and pepper to taste. The potatoes should be soft enough to fall off a spoon in a thick stream but not so loose that they're soupy. 3-5 minutes Texture is creamy with minimal lumps, potatoes hold soft peaks when mashed, mixture is warm throughout
  7. 7 Take the meatloaf out of the oven and let it rest in the baking dish for 3-5 minutes before slicing. This resting period lets the internal temperature stabilize and allows the proteins to relax slightly, which helps them reabsorb some of the juices that have pooled inside. If you cut into it immediately, those juices run out onto the plate instead of staying in the meat. Use a sharp, serrated knife and cut with gentle downward pressure rather than sawing back and forth, which tends to tear the meat and create ragged slices. 3-5 minutes Meatloaf is still quite hot but not steaming aggressively, juices have mostly reabsorbed, surface is set and slightly firm

📌 Pinterest Tags: #cat1761229889885 #Easypost #1hourmeals #post #cooking #foodie #homemade #delicious #ground #breadcrumbs #yellow

📌 Save this post to Pinterest!

Perfect for meal planning and sharing with friends

media:content.media:title: Meatloaf and Creamy Mashed Potatoes: A Weeknight Dinner That Actually Works
media:content.media:description: A straightforward meatloaf recipe that stays moist and flavorful, paired with properly creamy mashed potatoes. Learn the techniques that prevent dry meatloaf, how to achieve the right texture balance, and why this method produces consistent results every time.

media:group.media:keywords: cat1761229889885, Easypost, 1hourmeals, post, cooking, foodie, homemade, delicious, ground, breadcrumbs, yellow
